🇮🇶 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 American Journalist Shelly Kittleson Released After One Week Kataib Hezbollah (Islamic Resistance in Iraq) released 49-year-old American journalist Shelly Kittleson after holding her for one week. Kittleson is a reporter specializing in the West Asia, with over a decade covering Iraq, Syria, and Afghanistan. She has contributed to Al-Monitor, Foreign Policy, and Politico. Abu Mujahid al-Assaf announced the decision and posted a video of Kittleson recorded during her time in custody, in which she discussed her activities and information-gathering in Iraq. Conditions: She must leave Iraq immediately. The release included a quiet prisoner swap with detained resistance members. The resistance group called it a one-time goodwill gesture amid war with the Zionist-American enemy.

🇨🇳 🇮🇷 According to Associated Press , the two-week ceasefire between Washington and Tehran was made possible in part due to China’s involvement. As Iran’s largest trading partner, Beijing reportedly encouraged Tehran throughout negotiations to pursue a ceasefire path. AP cites two anonymous officials saying China “urged Iranians to find a way to a ceasefire as talks progressed.” The report adds that Beijing worked through intermediaries—Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt—to help facilitate a diplomatic breakthrough. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
